column1: Fill in: "Water _____ at 100 degrees Celsius." (boil / boils / is boiling)
column2:
boils
Present simple untuk fakta umum dan kebenaran ilmiah. Subjek tunggal (water) butuh akhiran -s.
Water boils at 100 degrees Celsius.
The Earth revolves around the Sun.

column1: Which is correct: "Look! It _____." (rains / is raining / has rained)
column2:
is raining
Present continuous untuk aksi yang sedang terjadi sekarang. Sering dipakai dengan "now", "look", "listen".
Look! It is raining heavily outside.
She is currently writing her thesis.

column1: Fill in: "While I _____ dinner, the phone rang." (cooked / was cooking / had cooked)
column2:
was cooking
Past continuous untuk aksi panjang yang diinterupsi aksi lain (past simple). Pola: while + past continuous, past simple.
While I was cooking dinner, the phone rang.
They were studying when the lights went out.

column1: Which is correct: "I _____ in Jakarta for 5 years." (live / have lived / am living)
column2:
have lived
Present perfect untuk durasi yang dimulai di masa lalu dan masih berlangsung. Pakai "for" untuk durasi, "since" untuk titik waktu.
I have lived in Jakarta for 5 years.
She has worked here since 2020.

column1: Fill in: "She _____ to Bali last summer." (has gone / went / had gone)
column2:
went
Past simple untuk aksi selesai di waktu tertentu di masa lalu. Tanda: yesterday, last year, in 2020, ago.
She went to Bali last summer.
The researchers conducted the study in 2019.

column1: Which is correct: "I _____ this book for two hours and I'm still not finished." (read / have read / have been reading)
column2:
have been reading
Present perfect continuous untuk menekankan durasi aksi yang masih berlangsung atau baru selesai.
I have been reading this book for two hours.
He has been studying English since childhood.

column1: Fill in: "When I arrived, they _____ already _____." (had / left)
column2:
had already left
Past perfect (had + V3) untuk aksi yang terjadi SEBELUM aksi lain di masa lalu.
When I arrived, they had already left.
She had finished her work before the deadline.

column1: Which is correct for a planned arrangement: "I _____ my friend tomorrow at 7pm." (will meet / am meeting / meet)
column2:
am meeting
Present continuous untuk rencana masa depan yang sudah pasti/sudah diatur. "Will" untuk keputusan spontan; "going to" untuk niat.
I am meeting my friend tomorrow at 7pm.
We are flying to London next week.

column1: Fill in: "Look at those clouds! It _____ rain." (will / is going to / is)
column2:
is going to
"Going to" untuk prediksi berdasar bukti yang terlihat sekarang. "Will" untuk prediksi tanpa bukti.
Look at those clouds! It is going to rain.
I think the economy will improve next year.

column1: Which is correct (passive): "The report _____ by the committee yesterday." (reviewed / was reviewed / has reviewed)
column2:
was reviewed
Passive voice past simple: was/were + V3. Dipakai saat fokus pada objek, bukan pelaku.
The report was reviewed by the committee yesterday.
The novel was written in 1925.

column1: Fill in (passive present perfect): "The new policy _____ recently _____." (has / been implemented)
column2:
has been implemented
Passive present perfect: has/have + been + V3. Untuk aksi pasif yang baru selesai atau berdampak sekarang.
The new policy has been implemented recently.
Several solutions have been proposed.

column1: Fill in narrative: "She walked into the room. The lights _____ off and someone _____ behind the door." (turned / was hiding)
column2:
turned / was hiding
Narrative tenses: past simple untuk aksi utama, past continuous untuk latar belakang yang sedang berlangsung.
The lights turned off and someone was hiding behind the door.
It was raining when the accident happened.

column1: Which is correct: "I _____ smoke, but I quit last year." (used to / would / am used to)
column2:
used to
"Used to" untuk kebiasaan masa lalu yang sudah tidak dilakukan. "Would" hanya untuk aksi berulang, bukan keadaan. "Be used to" = terbiasa.
I used to smoke, but I quit last year.
She used to live in Surabaya.

column1: Fill in: "_____ as soon as I _____ home, I'll call you." (When / arrive)
column2:
As soon as I arrive
Time clauses (when, while, as soon as, before, after, until) pakai present simple untuk merujuk masa depan, BUKAN "will".
As soon as I arrive home, I will call you.
I will wait until she finishes.

column1: Which is correct: "By 2030, scientists _____ a cure for many diseases." (will find / will have found / will be finding)
column2:
will have found
Future perfect (will have + V3) untuk aksi yang akan selesai sebelum titik waktu di masa depan.
By 2030, scientists will have found a cure for many diseases.
She will have graduated by next June.

column1: Fill in: "_____ Sun rises in _____ east." (The / the / —)
column2:
The Sun / the east
Pakai "the" untuk benda unik (sun, moon, sky, internet) dan arah mata angin.
The Sun rises in the east.
The Moon orbits the Earth.

column1: Which is correct: "_____ books are important for education." (The / A / —)
column2:
— (zero article)
Tanpa artikel untuk noun jamak/uncountable yang bermakna umum (semua buku, semua orang).
Books are important for education.
Water is essential for life.

column1: Fill in: "I saw _____ dog yesterday. _____ dog was very friendly." (a / The)
column2:
a dog / The dog
First mention pakai "a/an" (belum spesifik). Second mention pakai "the" (sudah dikenal pembaca).
I saw a dog yesterday. The dog was very friendly.
She bought a book. The book was about history.

column1: Which is correct: "Mount Everest is _____ highest mountain in the world." (a / the / —)
column2:
the
Pakai "the" sebelum superlative dan untuk benda unik (only one of its kind).
Mount Everest is the highest mountain in the world.
This is the most interesting book I have read.

column1: Fill in: "She is _____ honest person and _____ university student." (an / a)
column2:
an honest / a university
"A/an" tergantung BUNYI, bukan huruf. "Honest" diawali bunyi vokal (h diam) → an. "University" diawali bunyi /ju/ konsonan → a.
She is an honest person.
He is a university student.

column1: Which is correct: "He lives in _____ United Kingdom, but his wife is from _____ Japan." (the / —)
column2:
the United Kingdom / — Japan
Pakai "the" untuk negara berbentuk plural/persatuan (the UK, the USA, the Netherlands). Tanpa artikel untuk negara biasa.
He lives in the United Kingdom.
His wife is from Japan.

column1: Fill in: "_____ education is _____ key to success." (— / the)
column2:
— Education / the key
Abstract nouns (education, love, freedom) tanpa artikel saat bermakna umum. Pakai "the" jika spesifik.
Education is the key to success.
Freedom is a fundamental human right.

column1: Which is correct: "I play _____ piano and _____ football." (the / —)
column2:
the piano / — football
Pakai "the" untuk alat musik. TANPA artikel untuk olahraga dan makanan.
I play the piano and football.
She plays the violin every Sunday.

column1: Fill in: "_____ Amazon is _____ longest river in South America." (The / the)
column2:
The Amazon / the longest
Pakai "the" untuk sungai, samudra, gurun, gunung berantai. Tidak untuk gunung tunggal atau danau.
The Amazon is the longest river in South America.
The Sahara is a vast desert.

column1: Which is correct: "She is _____ best student in _____ class." (the / the)
column2:
the best / the class
"The" sebelum superlative dan sebelum noun yang sudah jelas konteksnya (kelas yang dibicarakan).
She is the best student in the class.
This is the most important chapter.

column1: Fill in (zero conditional): "If you _____ ice, it _____." (heat / melts)
column2:
heat / melts
Zero conditional: if + present simple, present simple. Untuk fakta umum/kebenaran ilmiah.
If you heat ice, it melts.
If water reaches 100 degrees, it boils.

column1: Fill in (first conditional): "If it _____ tomorrow, we _____ the picnic." (rains / will cancel)
column2:
rains / will cancel
First conditional: if + present simple, will + V1. Untuk situasi nyata/mungkin di masa depan.
If it rains tomorrow, we will cancel the picnic.
If she studies hard, she will pass the exam.

column1: Fill in (second conditional): "If I _____ rich, I _____ a school for poor children." (were / would build)
column2:
were / would build
Second conditional: if + past simple, would + V1. Untuk situasi tidak nyata/khayalan di masa kini. Pakai "were" untuk semua subjek.
If I were rich, I would build a school.
If she had more time, she would travel more.

column1: Fill in (third conditional): "If she _____ harder, she _____ the scholarship." (had studied / would have got)
column2:
had studied / would have got
Third conditional: if + past perfect, would have + V3. Untuk penyesalan masa lalu (tidak bisa diubah).
If she had studied harder, she would have got the scholarship.
If we had left earlier, we would have caught the train.

column1: Fill in (mixed conditional): "If I _____ studied medicine, I _____ a doctor now." (had / would be)
column2:
had studied / would be
Mixed conditional: kondisi masa lalu (past perfect) → akibat masa kini (would + V1).
If I had studied medicine, I would be a doctor now.
If she had taken the job, she would be in London now.

column1: Which is correct: "If I _____ you, I would apologise." (was / were / am)
column2:
were
Dalam second conditional, pakai "were" untuk SEMUA subjek (I, he, she, it). Bentuk subjunctive.
If I were you, I would apologise immediately.
If he were here, he would help us.

column1: Fill in: "_____ you study, you will fail." (Unless / If not)
column2:
Unless
"Unless" = "if not". Tidak boleh digabung dengan "not" lagi. Salah: "Unless you don't study".
Unless you study, you will fail.
I won't go unless you come with me.

column1: Fill in (mixed conditional reverse): "If she _____ Spanish, she _____ that job last year." (spoke / would have got)
column2:
spoke / would have got
Mixed conditional reverse: kondisi masa kini (umum/permanen) → akibat masa lalu.
If she spoke Spanish, she would have got that job.
If he were taller, he would have been selected.

column1: Fill in (time prepositions): "The meeting is _____ Monday _____ 9am _____ January." (on / at / in)
column2:
on Monday / at 9am / in January
AT untuk jam. ON untuk hari/tanggal. IN untuk bulan/tahun/musim.
The meeting is on Monday at 9am in January.
She was born on 5 May at noon in 1995.

column1: Fill in (place prepositions): "The book is _____ the table _____ the living room _____ 23 Sunset Street." (on / in / at)
column2:
on the table / in the living room / at 23 Sunset Street
AT untuk titik/alamat. ON untuk permukaan. IN untuk ruang tertutup.
The book is on the table in the living room.
I live at 23 Sunset Street in Jakarta.

column1: Fill in: "Success depends _____ hard work and is closely related _____ effort." (on / to)
column2:
depends ON / related TO
Verb + preposition spesifik harus dihafal. "Depend on" (bergantung pada), "related to" (berhubungan dengan).
Success depends on hard work.
This issue is closely related to climate change.

column1: Fill in: "She is interested _____ history and good _____ mathematics." (in / at)
column2:
interested IN / good AT
Adjective + preposition: interested IN, good AT, afraid OF, proud OF, married TO, fond OF.
She is interested in history and good at mathematics.
He is proud of his achievements.

column1: Fill in: "Water _____ hydrogen and oxygen, and we should focus _____ the issue." (consists of / on)
column2:
consists OF / focus ON
"Consist of" = terdiri dari (jangan pakai "consist from"). "Focus on" = fokus pada.
Water consists of hydrogen and oxygen.
We should focus on the main issue.

column1: Fill in: "She had to cope _____ many difficulties." (with / for / on)
column2:
cope WITH
"Cope with" = mengatasi/menghadapi. Other: deal with, struggle with, agree with someone.
She had to cope with many difficulties.
We must deal with this problem urgently.

column1: Fill in: "I usually go to work _____ car, but yesterday I went _____ foot." (by / on)
column2:
by car / on foot
BY + means of transport (by car, by bus, by train, by plane). Tapi: ON foot, ON horseback.
I usually go to work by car.
Yesterday I went on foot.

column1: Fill in: "He is afraid _____ spiders but fond _____ cats." (of / of)
column2:
afraid OF / fond OF
Banyak adjective pakai "of": afraid of, fond of, aware of, capable of, jealous of, tired of.
He is afraid of spiders but fond of cats.
I am aware of the consequences.

column1: Correct the error: "There are many informations in this article."
column2:
much information
"Information" adalah uncountable noun (tidak bisa dijamakkan). Pakai "much/a lot of/some", BUKAN "many" atau "-s".
There is much information in this article.
She gave me a lot of information.

column1: Correct the error: "He gave me some good advices."
column2:
some good advice
"Advice" uncountable. Untuk satuan: "a piece of advice". Sama untuk: equipment, furniture, knowledge, research.
He gave me some good advice.
She offered a piece of advice.

column1: Correct the error: "The childrens are playing in the park."
column2:
The children are playing
"Children" sudah jamak (irregular plural dari child). Tidak boleh ditambah -s. Sama: people, men, women, feet, teeth.
The children are playing in the park.
Many people attended the conference.

column1: Correct the error: "I have lived here since 5 years ago."
column2:
for 5 years / 5 years ago
"Since" untuk titik waktu (since 2020, since Monday). "For" untuk durasi. "Ago" pakai past simple, bukan present perfect.
I have lived here for 5 years.
I moved here 5 years ago.

column1: Correct the error: "He very smart and tall."
column2:
He IS very smart and tall
Bahasa Indonesia tidak punya copula (to be). Bahasa Inggris WAJIB pakai is/am/are sebelum adjective.
He is very smart and tall.
The students are intelligent.

column1: Correct the error: "I and my friend went to the library."
column2:
My friend and I went
Sopan santun: orang lain disebut dulu, "I" terakhir. Pakai "I" sebagai subjek, "me" sebagai objek.
My friend and I went to the library.
She invited my friend and me.

column1: Correct the error: "We will discuss about the problem tomorrow."
column2:
We will discuss the problem
"Discuss" adalah transitive verb — TIDAK butuh preposisi. Sama: enter (a room), marry (someone), reach (a place).
We will discuss the problem tomorrow.
She entered the building quietly.

column1: Correct the error: "She is married with a doctor."
column2:
married TO a doctor
"Married to" (bukan "with"). Sama: similar to, related to, attached to, addicted to.
She is married to a doctor.
This case is similar to the previous one.

column1: Correct the error: "This phone is more cheaper than that one."
column2:
cheaper / more cheap (no — just "cheaper")
JANGAN double comparative. Pilih SATU: -er ATAU more, tidak dua-duanya.
This phone is cheaper than that one.
This book is more interesting than that one.

column1: Correct the error: "Math is the most easiest subject for me."
column2:
the easiest
JANGAN double superlative. Pilih SATU: -est ATAU most.
Math is the easiest subject for me.
This is the most beautiful place I've seen.

column1: Correct the error: "The furniture in this office are very modern."
column2:
The furniture IS very modern
"Furniture" uncountable singular → pakai IS. Sama: information, advice, equipment, news, research, luggage.
The furniture in this office is very modern.
The news is shocking.

column1: Form comparative: "This problem is _____ (difficult) than the last one."
column2:
more difficult
Adjective 2+ suku kata: pakai "more". Adjective 1 suku kata: pakai -er. Adjective 2 suku kata berakhir -y: ganti y → ier.
This problem is more difficult than the last one.
She is happier now than before.

column1: Form superlative: "Mount Everest is the _____ (high) mountain in the world."
column2:
highest
Adjective 1 suku kata: tambah -est. Adjective 2+ suku kata: pakai "the most". Selalu pakai "the" sebelum superlative.
Mount Everest is the highest mountain.
This is the most important issue.

column1: Form irregular: "good → _____ → _____. bad → _____ → _____."
column2:
good → better → best. bad → worse → worst
Irregular: good/better/best, bad/worse/worst, far/farther/farthest, little/less/least, much/more/most.
This is the best book I have ever read.
Things are getting worse every day.

column1: Fill in: "He is _____ tall _____ his brother." (as / as)
column2:
as tall as
"As + adjective + as" untuk menyatakan kesetaraan. Negatif: "not as ... as" atau "not so ... as".
He is as tall as his brother.
This task is not as difficult as I thought.

column1: Fill in: "_____ more you study, _____ better your results." (The / the)
column2:
The more / the better
"The + comparative, the + comparative" untuk menyatakan paralel berubah. Pola formal IELTS Writing.
The more you study, the better your results.
The harder we work, the more we earn.

column1: Which is correct: "I _____ go now. The rules require it." (must / have to / should)
column2:
have to / must
"Must" = kewajiban dari pembicara sendiri. "Have to" = kewajiban dari luar (aturan, hukum). Negatif berbeda: "mustn't" (dilarang) vs "don't have to" (tidak perlu).
I have to go now. The rules require it.
You must finish this report today.

column1: Which is correct: "You _____ see a doctor about that cough." (should / must / can)
column2:
should
"Should" untuk saran/rekomendasi. "Ought to" lebih formal, makna sama. "Must" terlalu kuat untuk saran biasa.
You should see a doctor about that cough.
Students ought to read widely.

column1: Fill in: "She _____ swim when she was 4. Now she _____ swim 100m without stopping." (could / can / is able to)
column2:
could / can / is able to
"Could" = kemampuan umum di masa lalu. "Can" = kemampuan sekarang. "Be able to" untuk tense lain (future, perfect).
She could swim when she was 4.
Now she is able to swim 100m without stopping.

column1: Which is correct: "It _____ rain later. Bring an umbrella just in case." (may / must / should)
column2:
may / might
"May/might" = kemungkinan (50/50). "Could" juga bisa. "Must" = kepastian/kesimpulan logis.
It may rain later. Bring an umbrella.
She might be at home now.
